fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: High-growth company with impressive revenue expansion and strong analyst backing, though valuation metrics are stretched.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 4.6% (EPS 2.37 vs 2.27); next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates for current Q (236.6%) and FY (300.7%) are extremely high.
Operating margin 21.8% and Profit margin 17.7% indicate strong profitability.
Return on equity 22.8% shows efficient use of shareholder capital.
Consensus 'buy' rating from 24 analysts with a high mean target price of $1104.68.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 160.52 and Forward P/E of 50.02 are very high, indicating a premium valuation.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 28.31 is also exceptionally high.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44 suggests significant leverage.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $93.35M is small relative to market cap and debt.
Beta of 1.53 indicates higher volatility than the broader market.

news.paragraph: Lumentum (LITE) has seen mixed signals recently, with a bullish Seeking Alpha piece highlighting its strong year-to-date returns and a rating upgrade tied to exploding purchase obligations, while other reports note a sector-wide cooling off with LITE down 4% on May 29th. Additionally, a registered bearish activity by Leopold Aschenbrenner adds a note of caution. Despite these crosscurrents, analyst consensus remains a strong buy with a high average target price, and the company recently beat its last earnings estimate.
